verses.text: Jotham reigns and does right as his father had, yet 'he entered not into the temple of the LORD' — avoiding Uzziah's sin — though the people still acted corruptly. He learns from a father's fall. A wise son marks where his father stumbled and is careful not to follow.

Jotham reigns and does right as his father had, yet 'he entered not into the temple of the LORD' — avoiding Uzziah's sin — though the people still acted corruptly. He learns from a father's fall. A wise son marks where his father stumbled and is careful not to follow.

Jotham builds the high gate of the temple, fortifies the wall, and raises cities and castles in the hills. He labours steadily to strengthen his realm. A quiet, building reign that adds and repairs is its own kind of faithfulness.

Jotham builds the high gate of the temple, fortifies the wall, and raises cities and castles in the hills. He labours steadily to strengthen his realm. A quiet, building reign that adds and repairs is its own kind of faithfulness.

He defeats the Ammonites and exacts heavy tribute, for 'Jotham became mighty, because he prepared his ways before the LORD his God.' His strength is rooted in a settled walk with God. Ordering our ways before the LORD is the true preparation for every success.

He defeats the Ammonites and exacts heavy tribute, for 'Jotham became mighty, because he prepared his ways before the LORD his God.' His strength is rooted in a settled walk with God. Ordering our ways before the LORD is the true preparation for every success.

The rest of Jotham's acts are recorded; he reigns sixteen years, dies, and Ahaz his son succeeds him. A good but brief reign closes. The faithful pass on, and the throne tests whether the next heart will keep or forsake the LORD.
